fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Fixing x:A, we must construct an identification from H \mathopen {} \left ( f x \right ) \mathclose {} to \mathsf {act}_{ f } \mathopen {} \left ( H x \right ) \mathclose {}. By the naturality () of H at the identification H x \colon f x = x, we have an identification H \mathopen {} \left ( f x \right ) \mathclose {} \cdot \mathsf {act}_{ 1_{ A } } \mathopen {} \left ( H x \right ) \mathclose {} = \mathsf {act}_{ f } \mathopen {} \left ( H x \right ) \mathclose {} \cdot H x.

Simplifying, we have H \mathopen {} \left ( f x \right ) \mathclose {} \cdot H x = \mathsf {act}_{ f } \mathopen {} \left ( H x \right ) \mathclose {} \cdot H x; canceling H x on either side, we therefore have the desired identification H \mathopen {} \left ( f x \right ) \mathclose {} = \mathsf {act}_{ f } \mathopen {} \left ( H x \right ) \mathclose {}.

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Naturality of homotopies onto the identity

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Let H \colon f \sim 1_{ A } be a homotopy from f \colon A \to A onto the identity function. Then we may construct a higher homotopy from H \circ f to \mathsf {act}_{ f } \circ H.

Fixing x:A, we must construct an identification from H \mathopen {} \left ( f x \right ) \mathclose {} to \mathsf {act}_{ f } \mathopen {} \left ( H x \right ) \mathclose {}. By the naturality () of H at the identification H x \colon f x = x, we have an identification H \mathopen {} \left ( f x \right ) \mathclose {} \cdot \mathsf {act}_{ 1_{ A } } \mathopen {} \left ( H x \right ) \mathclose {} = \mathsf {act}_{ f } \mathopen {} \left ( H x \right ) \mathclose {} \cdot H x.

Simplifying, we have H \mathopen {} \left ( f x \right ) \mathclose {} \cdot H x = \mathsf {act}_{ f } \mathopen {} \left ( H x \right ) \mathclose {} \cdot H x; canceling H x on either side, we therefore have the desired identification H \mathopen {} \left ( f x \right ) \mathclose {} = \mathsf {act}_{ f } \mathopen {} \left ( H x \right ) \mathclose {}.

Let H \colon f \sim g be a homotopy between two functions f,g \colon A \to B. For any identification p \colon x =y in A, we have an identification H x \cdot \mathsf {act}_{ g } \, p = \mathsf {act}_{ f } \, p \cdot Hy.
By identification induction on p, it suffices to show that Hx \cdot \mathsf {refl} = \mathsf {refl} \cdot Hx.
